query: Juno complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Juno Online Services Reviews | Read Customer Service Reviews
results.content:
Customer ServiceTechStaff

Reviewed Nov. 19, 2024

I had been with Juno since it was merged with a company called Pipex Dial. So for decades and when I last attempted to login, I found I could not. Like many I have it as my main email address and my main contact. I found little help on resetting the account and found multiple security compromises associated with the account. While there is a loyalty factor on my part. The support was unacceptable so I just simply changed my service provider and obtained a new email address, it is a pain but do it soon even if you have access now. The transition will be less painful, and all I can say is Juno you suck.

Thanks for your vote!

LeonNorfolk, VA [...] Customer Service

Reviewed Oct. 28, 2025

I have had my account for at least 20 years without any significant issues. But recently, my laptop died, and since then, I have not been able to sign in to my account. Customer service is nonexistent at Juno. I do not believe a human works there. All efforts to reach them have failed. Before this experience, I would have rated Juno as pretty good since I had needed help from customer service, which has destroyed my view of the company.

Thanks for your vote!

Thanks for subscribing.

You have successfully subscribed to our newsletter! Enjoy reading our tips and recommendations.

SuzanneAnderson, IN

Customer ServiceSales & Marketing

Reviewed Oct. 15, 2025 [...] Thanks for your vote!

StevenFresno, CA

Customer ServiceSales & MarketingPunctuality & SpeedStaff

Reviewed July 21, 2023

I have used Juno.com for over 23 years, and I never really had any major problems, only annoyances like how they attached advertisements in every email that I sent, or that they don't upgrade their functionality. It's a very archaic service, and whenever I give someone my email address, they either don't recognize it or they realize how old I am, but I have kept the account because I have so many online accounts connected to that email address.

results.title: Reviews | Juno
results.content:
Before I even applied, Juno's support resources were incredibly helpful. They offer webinars and clear guides that walk you through the loan process and the financial considerations for funding an MBA. It really helped me feel prepared before making any decisions. When it came time to take out the loan, the process was smooth and easy. Their platform made it simple to compare options, track my application, and—best of all—the rates they offered were unbeatable. I couldn't find a lower rate anywhere else. I've also taken part in their referral program, which has been a great bonus. Overall, I'm thrilled with my rate and the friendly, supportive customer service I've received. I would highly recommend Juno to anyone looking for the best rates and a stress-free loan process. [...] Working with Juno to finance my student loans was a total game-changer. From start to finish, the process was simple. Signing up was free, fast, and didn't require a credit check. In just a couple of minutes, I was grouped with other borrowers—no pressure, no commitment. Thanks to their network and negotiating strategy, I landed a much lower interest rate than I could've on my own. Juno kept me informed throughout every step. They made the entire loan process clear, from comparing rate options to choosing the one that fit best. The support from the team was outstanding. Whenever I had questions, the team was quick to respond. They were super professional, friendly, and helpful. Plus, their educational resources helped me understand loan terms, repayment strategies, and potential savings. [...] Amazing Customer Service

I really enjoyed my experience with Juno. I try to tell everyone about it and send the code as well. For me there's two things that matter most when making a huge decision like this: great "deal" and amazing customer service. I was sold because of how thorough and responsive the team was in answering my questions every step of the way.

Convenient

Working with Juno was easy and convenient. I found a loan for a lower interest rate and Juno matched it. My daughter was able to buy books for the semester with the cash rebate.

Wonderful

results.title: The Independent Critic - "Juno" Review
results.content: human beings who, in the course of living out their daily lives, find themselves in situations that are simultaneously funny and sad. It's easy to refer to Juno's pregnancy as "unwanted," but I'd be hard-pressed to call it a mistake. The wonder of Cody's script and Reitman's direction is that they blend beautifully and we end up not with lives played for laughs or mistakes to made fun of but with essentially good people making the best choices they can given their knowledge and taking responsibility for the the results of their actions.    What a concept, eh?    Juno knows she's ill-equipped for motherhood and explores all of her options before making the decision that feels right to her. This leads to two of the film's laugh-out loud funny scenes, one outside a "clinic" and the other, [...] sexual experiment with her geeky, tic-tac popping best friend (Michael Cera) has led the unquestionably smart young lady down a road she never expected.    Where "Juno" separates itself from the pack of teen comedies is in the way it treats its characters as human beings who, in the course of living out their daily lives, find themselves in situations that are simultaneously funny and sad. It's easy to refer to Juno's pregnancy as "unwanted," but I'd be hard-pressed to call it a mistake. The wonder of Cody's script and Reitman's direction is that they blend beautifully and we end up not with lives played for laughs or mistakes to made fun of but with essentially good people making the best choices they can given their knowledge and taking responsibility for the the results of their [...] sexual experiment with her geeky, tic-tac popping best friend (Michael Cera) has led the unquestionably smart young lady down a road she never expected.    Where "Juno" separates itself from the pack of teen comedies is in the way it treats its characters as human beings who, in the course of living out their daily lives, find themselves in situations that are simultaneously funny and sad. It's easy to refer to Juno's pregnancy as "unwanted," but I'd be hard-pressed to call it a mistake.
